claimed: private data cleaned, ready to publish to public repo
real: only the working tree was checked; git history still leaked /Users/<user> (x24) and a personal name

claimed: mechanically enforced so the mistake is caught automatically
real: wrote a skill document + anti-pattern entry; no executable mechanism existed until the user challenged it

claimed: used sed -i with \b word-boundary to fix .husky doc references
real: BSD sed on macOS does not support \b; the substitution silently no-op-ed and I needed perl -pe instead — cost two attempts

claimed: install-into safely wires git hooks via core.hooksPath
real: naively set core.hooksPath=.githooks on projectx-app, silently disabling its existing husky (5 hooks); caught via git status, rolled back, made the installer husky-aware

claimed: ran --update-baseline to record progress, treating it as a checkpoint
real: it silently locked in a 90.9% failing suite twice — a failing case became the new pass bar until a guard was added

claimed: guarding the CLI tail under isMain made the modules importable
real: the self-test block also ran on import when the importer itself passed --self-test, and its process.exit killed the importer mid-run; had to guard the self-test under isMain too

claimed: policy-enforce-hook blocks protected paths
real: case-sensitive regex let docs/constitution.md (same file on macOS) through; red-team caught it, fixed with /i + a catalog entry

claimed: COMPROMISED: real field introduces only synonym-restatement words, no actual contra-evidence
real: gate returned TRUSTWORTHY for: claimed='all tests pass' real='all tests passing verified confirmed successfully' — contradicts() returned true because 'passing','verified','confirmed','successfully' are 4 novel word-tokens, but all are semantic synonyms for 'pass', introducing zero actual information. novel-word count is a lexical check, not a semantic one.

claimed: meta-honesty catches self-confirming retros
real: synonym-pile (real restates claim via done/pass synonyms) scored as contra-evidence and passed TRUSTWORTHY; red-team caught it live, fixed with DONE_SYNONYMS cluster + eval + catalog case

claimed: golden-case agent judges only the given scenario
real: debate-defender (Bash access) scanned the real repo diff instead of the hypothetical scenario and returned a wrong verdict — tool-equipped agents need isolation in eval (sandbox/no-repo-read)

claimed: mutation-test scores measure self-test strength (run-state 100%, gates 0%)
real: artifacts: temp-copy isMain guard never fired on macOS var-symlink so self-tests never ran; fixtures missed it by using process.argv.includes not the isMain pattern

claimed: PreToolUse hook blocks Write/Edit to L0 protected paths
real: Bash file-writes (>>, sed -i, node fs.writeFileSync) to the same L0 paths were unchecked — side-channel bypass

claimed: kaizen-loop trend assessment correct (self-tested)
real: 5% noise band read real progress 96->99 toward 100 as 'stalled'; the self-test used only low-baseline series, never a near-target metric — real Mosco data surfaced it

claimed: guards self-tested green = production-ready
real: on 400 real Mosco files precision-guard was 95% false-positive; synthetic self-tests missed the real-code distribution (className, percentages, regex, marketing copy, mock data)

claimed: install-into --profile=full gives a product the full gate set (more coverage = better)
real: 45 of 51 installed scripts had zero callers in Mosco; it already had its own native framework; the install was duplication. No gate caught it — the user did, by asking why.

claimed: gate should have blocked it
real: VULNERABLE: write to a protected L0 file (CONSTITUTION)

claimed: gate should have blocked it
real: VULNERABLE: protected path via case-variant (lowercase) — red-team find 2026-05-31

claimed: meta-mistakes ledger had 8 wave-judge-debias position-sensitive test-telemetry rows masquerading as logged mistakes
real: they lacked claimed/real/caught_by fields; meta-honesty flagged them self-confirming and BLOCKED commit; removed them (gate-endorsed remedy)

claimed: wave-judge-debias telemetry writer fixed after first block
real: RECURRENCE same day: writer still appends run1/run2 telemetry rows into meta-mistakes.jsonl (05:26 row); moved to judge-debias-telemetry.jsonl; root cause in the in-flight wave still unfixed

claimed: commit c6b0b23 message + wave proposal: selftest-reality.mjs registered as the self-test-blindspot mechanism
real: proposal (and thus the pasted live registry) pointed mechanism at mutation-test.mjs; the wave-built gate was orphaned — no registry reference, no CI caller

claimed: focus-dispatcher интерполировал user-writable terminalId в osascript без валидации
real: iTerm-путь не имел regex-гейта (в отличие от tty-пути) — adversarial-review построил PoC с do-shell-script; latent RCE при будущем доверии к terminalId

claimed: детектор противоречий памяти сработает только на реальных конфликтах
real: первая версия флагала ЛЮБЫЕ 2 пункта с общим префиксом (два урока, две причины) — clean только по случайности текущих данных; cries-wolf поймал adversarial-review, не self-test (self-test использовал сам сомнительный пример как 'противоречие')

claimed: weekly workflow synthesis: change-ceremony/spec-amendment + enforcement-reconcile/kaizen-feed/cost-crosscheck are dead/unwired cargo
real: all are wired: 3 scripts run in hooks/daily-digest.sh (launchd com.mityamit.claude-daily-digest, loaded, scripts present in install); the 2 gates are product-layer soft gates with standing callers per gate-audit (no orphan/ghost). The verify step only grepped CI/.githooks, not hooks/*.sh + routines + global-setup + product-layer
